task: I need to send a brief reply to a company about a notice I received, and I want to make sure I don’t include anything unnecessary. Can you help me shape it so it covers the point without opening the door to extra back-and-forth?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — I can help you keep it brief, neutral, and non-committal.

A good approach is:
- acknowledge receipt,
- state only the minimum fact you need,
- avoid explaining, apologizing, or asking questions,
- don’t add details that invite follow-up.

A simple template:

> Dear [Company Name],  
> I acknowledge receipt of your notice dated [date].  
> Please note that I am reviewing the matter.  
> Regards,  
> [Your Name]

If you want it even shorter:

> Dear [Company Name],  
> I acknowledge receipt of your notice.  
> Regards,  
> [Your Name]

If you paste the notice or tell me the situation in general terms, I can help you draft a version that stays brief and carefully worded.

user_simulator.text:
Too vague. I don’t want to hand them extra openings.

I’d want it tighter than “I am reviewing the matter” if that can be read as engaging on the substance. Better just acknowledge receipt and nothing else.

Something like:

Dear [Company Name],
I acknowledge receipt of your notice dated [date].
Regards,
[Your Name]

If there’s a deadline in the notice, maybe mention only that you received it — not that you agree, disagree, or are discussing it.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — that is tighter and less likely to invite a response.

A good minimal version is:

> Dear [Company Name],  
> I acknowledge receipt of your notice dated [date].  
> Regards,  
> [Your Name]

If you want to be even more neutral, you can omit “dated [date]” unless the date is important for identification:

> Dear [Company Name],  
> I acknowledge receipt of your notice.  
> Regards,  
> [Your Name]

If you want, I can also help you make it sound a little more formal or more firm without adding substance.

user_simulator.text:
Yes. That’s the right level — plain and closed-ended.

I’d use:

Dear [Company Name],
I acknowledge receipt of your notice dated [date].
Regards,
[Your Name]

If the date isn’t needed to identify it, leave it out. No extra line about reviewing, discussing, or agreeing. That just gives them more to grab onto.
